aliases: Idealist Hearths Educational and Cultural Foundation
Ülkü Ocakları Eğitim ve Kültür Vakfı
description: The Grey Wolves are a Turkish far-right ultranationalist and neo-fascist political movement and the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP). Founded by Colonel Alparslan Türkeş in the late 1960s, they engaged in urban guerrilla warfare during the late 1970s political violence in Turkey and are implicated in numerous massacres and assassinations. The organization has been banned in Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, and France, and is active in Turkey, Northern Cyprus, Western Europe, and Central Asia.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 26 June 2020, Turkish nationalist groups, identified by journalist Jake Hanrahan as Grey Wolves members, attacked Kurdish rallies in Vienna...
country_links.context: Grey Wolves members attacked Kurdish rallies in Vienna.

country_links.evidence_quote: During the First Nagorno-Karabakh War (1988–94), Grey Wolves members fought on the Azerbaijani side against Armenians... Around 200 members of the Grey Wolves were still in the conflict zone in September 1994 to train Azerbaijani units.
country_links.context: Grey Wolves members fought on the Azerbaijani side during the First Nagorno-Karabakh War and trained Azerbaijani units.

country_links.evidence_quote: has affiliated branches in Western European nations with a significant Turkish diaspora such as Germany, Belgium and the Netherlands.
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves have affiliated branches in Belgium.

country_links.evidence_quote: they targeted the Uyghurs, concentrated in the Chinese province of Xinjiang and began actively supporting the East Turkestan independence movement.
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves targeted Uyghurs in Xinjiang and supported the East Turkestan independence movement.

country_links.evidence_quote: In May 1984, Grey Wolves leader Abdullah Çatlı carried out a bombing of an Armenian Genocide memorial in Alfortville, a Paris suburb.
country_links.context: Grey Wolves leader Abdullah Çatlı carried out a bombing of an Armenian Genocide memorial in Alfortville.

country_links.evidence_quote: has affiliated branches in Western European nations with a significant Turkish diaspora such as Germany... They are the largest right-wing extremist organization in Germany.
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves have affiliated branches in Germany and are the largest right-wing extremist organization there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Drugs were transported to Italy, where organized crime processed them.
country_links.context: Drugs were transported to Italy, where organized crime processed them, involving Grey Wolves leaders.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2005, Kazakhstan also banned the organization, classifying it as a terrorist group.
country_links.context: Kazakhstan banned the Grey Wolves in 2005, classifying it as a terrorist group.

country_links.evidence_quote: According to Wangmo and Yazilitas, the Grey Wolves in the Netherlands have engaged in a variety of activities, ranging from criminal activities and nationalist propaganda to support of football teams.
country_links.context: Grey Wolves organizations in the Netherlands have engaged in criminal activities and nationalist propaganda.

country_links.evidence_quote: In August 1996, the Grey Wolves were involved in an attack on a protest of Greek Cypriots against the Turkish occupation of Northern Cyprus.
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves were involved in violence, attacks, and political activities in Northern Cyprus.

country_links.evidence_quote: There is allegedly a Grey Wolf group recruiting in Drammen, Norway.
country_links.context: There is allegedly a Grey Wolf group recruiting in Drammen, Norway.

country_links.evidence_quote: Members of the Grey Wolves fought on the Chechen separatist side during the First Chechen War (1994–1996) and the Second Chechen War (1999–2000).
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves organized arms shipments to Chechnya and fought on the Chechen separatist side.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 13 September 2015, an explosion occurred at a Kurdish civil center in Stockholm, Sweden, following clashes between Turks, Kurds, and anti-fascists at a rally organized by the Swedish Grey Wolves.
country_links.context: An explosion occurred at a Kurdish civil center in Stockholm following clashes involving the Swedish Grey Wolves.

country_links.evidence_quote: Activists of the MHP and Idealist Hearths have fought in the Syrian civil war in support of the Syrian Turkmen, whom they consider kinsmen.
country_links.context: Grey Wolves members fought in the Syrian civil war supporting Syrian Turkmen and provided aid.

country_links.evidence_quote: The 2015 Bangkok bombing is suspected to have been carried out by the Grey Wolves due to Thailand's deportation of Uyghur terrorist suspects back to China...
country_links.context: The Grey Wolves are suspected of carrying out the 2015 Bangkok bombing.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 13 May 1981 Mehmet Ali Ağca, an alleged Grey Wolves member, attempted to murder Pope John Paul II in St. Peter's Square.
country_links.context: An alleged Grey Wolves member attempted to murder Pope John Paul II in St. Peter's Square.
